commentary: Six activities against last week's eight, and the swim volume dropped noticeably — two sessions at 1500m each compared to three at 2000m and a cheeky ocean dip at Balmoral on top of that. The heart rate tells its own story too: you were pushing into the 140s and 149 last week, and this week both swims sat at a steady 130, which either means you've found a more controlled pace or the effort just wasn't quite there, and only you know which. The weight training, though, is a different picture entirely — four lifting sessions in one week is the most structured gym block you've put together, and that kind of consistency is exactly how you stop just being fit and start looking like it. No running at all this week after that 13.3km Mosman circuit and the very quotation-marked recovery run last week, so honestly the rest was probably earned — just don't let the pool go quiet for too long, because those lanes at Prince Alfred have a certain energy that clearly agrees with you.
metrics_commentary: Three thousand metres in the pool and not a single step on land — honestly very on-brand, very mermaid energy, and he is not complaining. That HRV sitting at 58 against a seven-day average of 48 is the body basically announcing that it is recovered, rested, and ready to be absolutely feral, which is exciting news. The VO2 Max at 55.6 is genuinely impressive and doing a lot of heavy lifting this week given the running total of zero kilometres on foot, so maybe next week we lace up the sneakers — the cute ones — and take that engine out for a spin on dry land.
gym_commentary: Four for four, baby — Scott said four sessions and Matt did four sessions, which is exactly the kind of obedient energy that deserves to be celebrated loudly. The bench press PR is the real gossip this week: 35.5 kg estimated one-rep max, up from 34.8, which means those chest gains are moving in the right direction and Matt's body is quietly, steadily doing the work. Weekly volume came in at 4,228 kg against last week's 19,984 kg, which is a significant drop and worth being honest about — something shifted in how the week was programmed or loaded, and that's worth a conversation with Scott. But the strength numbers tell a different story: a 168 kg quad, 107.9 kg on the trap bar, 84 kg on the lat pulldown, 76 kg on the incline barbell — that is a body that is being built, and it shows.
